name: lsp-setup
tagline: Detects, installs, configures, and verifies Language Server Protocol (LSP) tooling for ~19 languages via bundled Bun scripts.
verdict: Well-structured, deterministic LSP setup workflow with no secret access, no exfiltration, and no hidden logic. Main risk: it instructs `curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash` to install Bun, a pipe-to-shell pattern that trusts a remote script at fetch time. Otherwise transparent, in-scope, and honest about failure states.

highlights.summary: Line 139: 'Run with Bun: `curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash`.' Classic pipe-curl-to-shell pattern — executes a remotely fetched script with no integrity check, even though it's the official Bun

Relies on curl-piped install of Bun from bun.sh (line 139) and unreviewed per-language install commands in reference files not present in this scan — moderate supply-chain trust dependency on external
No explicit 'do not use when' guidance in the description or body; a reader can't tell when this skill is the wrong tool (e.g., IDE-native LSP already configured).
